body_txt: This is a must-have effect in almost every zombie movie. The hero sees a person who is facing away. The hero approaches slowly (as does the camera), maybe trying to enter a conversation with that person. More often than not an unnerving music plays. When we come close enough, the person suddenly turns around, revealing decaying flesh, bloody teeth and a missing eye . Oh my God, it's a zombie! Cue the Scare Chord . Of course, the trope isn't limited to zombies. Multiple face distortions can be shown this way. Sometimes, a Face Framed in Shadow can be revealed in this way, generally with dramatic results . For even more effect, the injury/mutation/decay may be only on one side of their face , allowing them to draw out the suspense by showing the normal side first, before they fully turn to reveal the horror. Compare Peek-a-Boo Corpse . See also Emerging from the Shadows , Not a Zombie , Two-Faced , Game Face , Over-the-Shoulder Murder Shot . For another kind of turn to show an unexpected face, see Chair Reveal . Not to be confused with anything related to Face–Heel Turn or Heel–Face Turn (or a reveal involving a character being Good All Along , for that matter).
